summary: Full backend + frontend testing of Real Estate CRM. Backend 100% pass. Frontend mostly works with one critical bug: login error message not shown for wrong credentials due to 401 interceptor in api.ts redirecting to /login before UI can show error.

ui_bugs.issue: Wrong credentials do NOT show error message. Root cause: api.ts response interceptor catches ALL 401s and does window.location.href = '/login' (full page reload), which clears React state before setError() can render the message.

action_items: Fix api.ts 401 interceptor: exclude '/api/auth/login' from the global redirect. Only redirect to /login on 401 for authenticated routes, not the login endpoint itself. Example fix: if (error.response?.status === 401 && !error.config?.url?.includes('/auth/login')) { window.location.href = '/login'; }
critical_code_review_comments: api.ts (line 26-28): Global 401 interceptor redirects ALL 401 responses including /api/auth/login to /login via window.location.href. This causes page reload which wipes React state, so login error messages never display. Fix: exclude auth/login endpoint from redirect.

success_rate.frontend: 93% (13/14 features working, error message on wrong credentials broken)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend all green. Frontend works except login error message not visible. Check api.ts interceptor fix - after fix, test by entering wrong credentials (wrong@test.com / wrongpass) and verify error appears without page reload.

rca_of_issue.bug: Login error message not shown for wrong credentials
rca_of_issue.root_cause: api.ts response interceptor at line 26 catches ALL 401 responses and calls window.location.href = '/login'. This full page reload happens before the LoginPage catch block can call setError(). The page remounts fresh with no error.
rca_of_issue.reproduction: 1. Go to /login. 2. Fill wrong email/password. 3. Click Sign in. 4. 401 received from server. 5. Interceptor redirects to /login (full reload). 6. No error message displayed.
rca_of_issue.mitigation: In api.ts interceptor, add check: if (error.response?.status === 401 && !error.config?.url?.includes('/auth/login')) before the redirect.
